task_completion.description: FreeCAD is open and the Python Console is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is open, and the 'Python console' panel is active and visible (usually at the bottom of the screen).

task_completion.description: Python commands for creating shapes and boolean operations are entered in the console.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python console contains commands referencing 'Part.makeBox', 'Part.makeCylinder', and boolean operations like 'cut' or 'makeCut'.

task_completion.description: A rectangular base plate is generated in the 3D viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A flat rectangular 3D box (representing the 120x80x5mm plate) is visible in the FreeCAD 3D view.

task_completion.description: Five holes are cut completely through the base plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D model of the plate shows exactly 5 holes passing completely through the thickness of the plate: one in each of the four corners, and one larger hole in the center.

task_completion.description: The document is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title, tree view, or a save command in the console shows the file named 'motor_mount_plate.FCStd'. A console command like 'saveAs("/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/motor_mount_plate.FCStd")' or a save dialog confirming this path should be visible.

integrity.description: The part was created programmatically via the Python console, not manually via GUI tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console shows the history of the shape creation commands. The tree view should show the resulting scripted Part/Cut objects rather than a history of manual GUI-based PartDesign sketches and pads.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual FreeCAD GUI being used, not just a text editor or terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent executed the commands rather than just typing them without running.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console shows output or the '>>>' prompt advancing after commands, and the 3D viewport updates to reflect the generated geometry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target object is a single solid motor mounting plate. Dimensions: 120mm (x) x 80mm (y) x 5mm (z). It has 5 holes: four 4.2mm diameter holes at corners (8,8), (8,72), (112,8), (112,72) and one 8.4mm diameter hole at (60,40). Target volume is exactly 47445.8 mm³. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/motor_mount_plate.FCStd.
